issues.title: Outdated Server Software
evidence: Server software version is outdated: Apache/2.4.52 (Ubuntu)
remediation: Update to the latest stable release, e.g., Apache 2.4.56 or later.

issues.title: Weak User Credentials
evidence: User credentials are stored in plain text or without proper hashing/salting.
Some passwords do not meet the required complexity policy.
remediation: Hash and salt all user credentials, enforce strong password policies.

issues.title: Misconfigured HTTP Headers
evidence: Server does not send Strict-Transport-Security (HSTS) header.
Content-Type header is missing or incorrect.
remediation: Enable HSTS, set correct Content-Type headers for all responses.

issues.title: Sensitive Information in HTTP Traffic
evidence: HTTPS is not enforced on certain pages.
Cookies are sent over unencrypted connections.
remediation: Ensure all traffic is encrypted, enforce HTTPS everywhere.

issues.title: Unsecured Communication
evidence: HTTP/1.1 protocol is used instead of HTTP/2 or later.
Server does not support modern encryption protocols like TLS 1.3.
remediation: Upgrade to the latest HTTP version, implement TLS 1.3.

limitations: This assessment is based on static analysis and may miss dynamic vulnerabilities.
Some issues might be false positives or require context-specific validation.
